title: Varronia wagnerorum
intro: Varronia wagnerorum, also known as the Wagner's Varronia or False Pau'au, is a plant species in the hortia genus of flowering plants. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Varronia wagnerorum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Varronia wagnerorum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Varronia wagnerorum is classified under the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family.
